title: AlmgrenChrissModel with eta=0.5 placeholder and is_calibrated flag
pyright_warnings_note: 14 reportUnknownMemberType warnings on pytest.approx are an artefact of pytest's missing type stubs; pyrightconfig.json demotes reportUnknownMemberType to 'warning' (matches the 24 informational warnings noted in the W1.1 status file). No code-action suggestions produced.

pytest_regression.note: Baseline 411 reflects the W1.1 (394+5) + W1.2 (+12) total. W1.3 adds 23. Total: 434 passed, 15 skipped, 0 failed, 0 regressions. Existing 411 tests pass byte-for-byte: DEFAULT_CRYPTO_COSTS and DEFAULT_STOCK_COSTS still have impact_model=None, and the legacy 2-arg cost.total_cost(notional, side) call site in engine.py produces identical results.

placeholder_math.formula: impact = eta * sigma * sqrt(qty / adv) * price
placeholder_math.tolerance: 1e-9 absolute (float-exact, matches PRD W1.3 acceptance criterion #2)

cost_model_extension.new_total_cost_signature: total_cost(notional, side, *, adv=None, sigma=None, price=None, qty=None)
cost_model_extension.impact_term_unit: bps of notional (added when impact_model is set AND all 4 impact inputs are provided)
cost_model_extension.note: Adding impact_model=None as default keeps the existing 2-arg call in engine.py producing byte-identical results. The impact term activates only when the caller explicitly threads adv/sigma/price/qty through (future W3-4 work).

real_data_capture.note: W1.3 ships a pure-compute placeholder by design. W2 will calibrate eta from real ccxt public-trade prints. W3-4 story 3.7 will re-run meta-label generation if the W2-calibrated eta differs from this placeholder by more than 2x.

acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: src/kairon/backtest/impact.py created (177 lines). AlmgrenChrissModel is a frozen=True, slots=True dataclass with eta: float = 0.5, is_calibrated: bool = False. Validation in __post_init__: eta>0 and finite; is_calibrated must be True/False (identity check, rejects 0/1 ints). compute(price, qty, adv, sigma, *, side='buy') returns impact in price units; compute_bps(...) returns 10000*impact/price. Side Literal={'buy','sell'}; symmetric. Module is pure (no IO, no async).

tests/backtest/test_impact.py::test_placeholder_eta passes. Constructs AlmgrenChrissModel(eta=0.5, is_calibrated=False), asserts is_calibrated is False, asserts eta==0.5, asserts compute(50000, 1, 1000, 0.01) == 0.5*0.01*sqrt(0.001)*50000 within 1e-9 (exact float math), asserts compute_bps returns ~1.581138 bps within 1e-6, and re-asserts is_calibrated is False (placeholder marker sticky).

src/kairon/backtest/cost.py::CostModel extended with impact_model: AlmgrenChrissModel | None = field(default=None, compare=False). __post_init__ rejects non-AlmgrenChrissModel values via type() identity check. total_cost signature extended with optional adv, sigma, price, qty kwargs (default None); impact term is added in bps only when impact_model is set AND all 4 impact inputs are provided. Legacy 2-arg call from engine.py is untouched and produces byte-identical results.

uv run pytest --tb=short -q: 434 passed, 15 skipped, 0 failed. 0 regressions. DEFAULT_CRYPTO_COSTS.impact_model is None and DEFAULT_STOCK_COSTS.impact_model is None (verified by test_cost_model_default_unchanged). Legacy 2-arg total_cost(notional, side) call from engine.py continues to compute commission+slippage+half_spread with no impact term.

DEFAULT_ETA = 0.5 exported from impact.py. AlmgrenChrissModel() default constructor returns eta=0.5, is_calibrated=False. The W3-4 story 3.7 cost-ML re-work loop will key off this flag and the W2 calibration will flip it to True when a calibrated value is published.

notes: W1.3 ships the placeholder Almgren-Chriss model with eta=0.5 and is_calibrated=False. The model is a frozen dataclass (not pydantic) to match the existing project pattern in cost.py. The cost-model extension is fully backward-compatible: the impact term activates only when impact_model is set AND all 4 impact inputs (adv, sigma, price, qty) are provided to total_cost; the legacy 2-arg call site in engine.py is byte-identical. W2 will calibrate eta per asset class from real ccxt public-trade prints; the is_calibrated flag will flip at that time. W3-4 story 3.7 (cost-ML re-work loop) will re-run meta-label generation if the W2-calibrated eta drifts from this placeholder by more than 2x.
